phase1.explanation:
Byte streams handle raw binary data:

InputStream (base class for reading bytes):
• FileInputStream — reads bytes from a file

OutputStream (base class for writing bytes):
• FileOutputStream — writes bytes to a file

Key methods:
• read() — reads single byte (returns -1 at end)
• read(byte[] buffer) — reads bytes into array
• write(byte[] data) — writes bytes
• write(byte[] data, int offset, int length) — writes partial array

When to use byte streams:
• Reading/writing binary files (images, audio, videos)
• Any file where you need raw bytes

Important:
• Always close resources (use try-with-resources)
• read() returns -1 when end of file is reached
• Byte streams work with ANY file type

Byte vs Character streams:
• Byte streams: raw bytes, any file
• Character streams: text files, handles encoding

phase2.errorMessage: (No error shown — but resources leak! The file remains open.)

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates an array of bytes: {10, 20, 30, 40, 50}
2. Writes these bytes to a file called "numbers.bin" using FileOutputStream
3. Reads the bytes back using FileInputStream
4. Prints each byte with its decimal value

Use try-with-resources for both operations.
dialogueHints: Use while ((b = fis.read()) != -1) to read until end
phase3.openingDialogue: Byte streams — reading and writing raw data! 💾
